raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section advances an explicit contribution claim — selection-based aggregation departing from synthesis-based ensembles — which requires verifying that the thesis is accurately framed and the gap statement is coherent.","R1.2":"The three-stage pipeline (candidate generation → NMS at IoU 0.3 → learned contrastive selector) has sequential logical dependencies; the calibration criterion, K-selection mechanism, and union-of-survivors strategy each need internal consistency checks.","R1.3":"The quantitative claim of 0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan and the comparison to the Setio FROC benchmark must be traceable to the described experimental setup on 412 held-out LIDC-IDRI scans.","R1.4":"An explicit novelty claim is made against FROC-optimized probability-map averaging (Setio et al.); the delta characterization needs verification that the distinction is correctly and completely described.","R1.6":"The prose contains systematic issues: pervasive run-on sentences with no coordinating punctuation, inconsistent past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated'), and heavy clause stacking that impedes readability."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize writing and prose quality (sentence clarity, grammar, tense consistency, readability), or technical soundness (pipeline logic, claim-evidence alignment, novelty delta against cited baselines) — or both equally?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances an explicit contribution claim — selection-based aggregation departing from synthesis-based ensembles — which requires verifying that the thesis is accurately framed and the gap statement is coherent.
rationale.R1.2: The three-stage pipeline (candidate generation → NMS at IoU 0.3 → learned contrastive selector) has sequential logical dependencies; the calibration criterion, K-selection mechanism, and union-of-survivors strategy each need internal consistency checks.
rationale.R1.3: The quantitative claim of 0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan and the comparison to the Setio FROC benchmark must be traceable to the described experimental setup on 412 held-out LIDC-IDRI scans.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit novelty claim is made against FROC-optimized probability-map averaging (Setio et al.); the delta characterization needs verification that the distinction is correctly and completely described.
rationale.R1.6: The prose contains systematic issues: pervasive run-on sentences with no coordinating punctuation, inconsistent past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated'), and heavy clause stacking that impedes readability.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize writing and prose quality (sentence clarity, grammar, tense consistency, readability), or technical soundness (pipeline logic, claim-evidence alignment, novelty delta against cited baselines) — or both equally?
